sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: After SPACE, the player moved from (45, 30) to (48, 18) - the center of the X shape! The player jumped to the X shape center. Let me verify this and understand the shape cycling.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check where player is now
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
white = [n for n in after if n['color'] == 'W'][0]
print(f"Player now at: {white['boundary']}")

# Check what changed
before = previous_frame.segmentation['nodes']
white_before = [n for n in before if n['color'] == 'W'][0]
print(f"Player was at: {white_before['boundary']}")

# Check large shapes
large = [n for n in after if n['color'] == 'R' and n['pixels'] > 20]
for s in large:
    cols = [p[1] for p in s['boundary']]
    rows = [p[0] for p in s['boundary']]
    print(f"Large shape {s['pixels']}px: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Player now at: [[48, 18]]
Player was at: [[45, 30]]
Large shape 49px: rows 44-46, cols 9-51

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After SPACE:
1. Player moved from (45, 30) to (48, 18) - the center of the X shape
2. The two large shapes merged into one: now there's a single 49px shape spanning cols 9-51 (previously 25px at cols 9-29 and 23px at cols 31-51, totaling 48px, now 49px)
So SPACE doesn't just cycle between shapes - it MERGES them! The two shapes (X and diamond) are now connected into one large shape. The player is at the center of the X part (48, 18).
Wait, but 25 + 23 = 48, and now we have 49. The extra pixel might be the connection point or the player position.
Actually, looking more carefully, the shapes were previously separate (cols 9-29 and cols 31-51, with a gap at col 30 where the player was). After SPACE, they merged into one shape spanning cols 9-51. The player is now at (48, 18), which is the center of the X shape.
So SPACE seems to "activate" or "merge" the shapes. The player jumps to the center of the first shape (the X).
Let me try SPACE again to see if it cycles to the next shape or does something else.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try SPACE again to see what happens
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
